channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Appeals court allows Trump administration expanded use of speedy deportations
item.description: A federal appeals court on Tuesday allowed the Trump administration to resume carrying out speedy deportations of undocumented migrants throughout the United States, not just near the border.
item.content:encoded:
A federal appeals court on Tuesday allowed the Trump administration to resume carrying out speedy deportations of undocumented migrants throughout the United States, not just near the border.

(Image credit: Morgan Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Mamdani's political gamble pays off as his endorsed candidates sweep their primaries
item.description: All three progressive candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani — including two who took on Democratic incumbents — won their primaries in safe seats almost guaranteeing their election in November.
item.content:encoded:
All three progressive candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani — including two who took on Democratic incumbents — won their primaries in safe seats almost guaranteeing their election in November.

(Image credit: Kena Betancur)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Supreme Court rules that prison guards can't be sued for shaving Rastafarian's head
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Boaters compete in 750-mile race... with no engines
item.description: For the "Race to Alaska," intrepid boaters compete in a 750-mile no-engine boat race from Washington state to Alaska.
item.content:encoded: For the "Race to Alaska," intrepid boaters compete in a 750-mile no-engine boat race from Washington state to Alaska.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Morrison

item.title: Fraught state fair opens on the National Mall
item.description: The Great American State Fair begins in D.C. as part of the country's 250th anniversary celebrations. It's bringing hundreds of exhibits to the National Mall, though some state aren't participating.
item.content:encoded: The Great American State Fair begins in D.C. as part of the country's 250th anniversary celebrations. It's bringing hundreds of exhibits to the National Mall, though some state aren't participating.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Portugal's Ronaldo shakes off World Cup doubters, scores 2 after sluggish start
item.description: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.
item.content:encoded:
At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.

(Image credit: Michael Steele)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: A Revolutionary War soldier's DNA links him to living relatives
item.description: Continental Army soldier John Pumphrey enlisted as a teenager in 1777 and fought at significant battles before his death in action against the British in Camden, S.C.
item.content:encoded:
Continental Army soldier John Pumphrey enlisted as a teenager in 1777 and fought at significant battles before his death in action against the British in Camden, S.C.

(Image credit: Jeffrey Collins)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: People fired over Charlie Kirk posts get big payouts for First Amendment retaliation
item.description: While workers, who were employees in government or public institutions, feel vindicated by how their lawsuits concluded, they are still grappling with the aftermath.
item.content:encoded:
While workers, who were employees in government or public institutions, feel vindicated by how their lawsuits concluded, they are still grappling with the aftermath.

(Image credit: Kathryn Gamble for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: How Trump's 'Complete and Total Endorsement' has reshaped the Republican Party
item.description: An NPR analysis of more than a thousand Trump endorsements in House, Senate and governor races over the last decade finds the president now picks candidates earlier — and in safer races.
item.content:encoded:
An NPR analysis of more than a thousand Trump endorsements in House, Senate and governor races over the last decade finds the president now picks candidates earlier — and in safer races.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: As Republican Party looks to future without Trump in office, Utah could be a road map
item.description: In 2028, President Trump will not be on the ballot, leaving Republicans to decide the future of the party. Utah — which has a complicated relationship with the president — could be a starting point.
item.content:encoded:
In 2028, President Trump will not be on the ballot, leaving Republicans to decide the future of the party. Utah — which has a complicated relationship with the president — could be a starting point.

(Image credit: Rick Egan)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller
